play_store_reviews.text: A very good organization app. I primarily use it for my to-do lists and organizing other personal info, but I have also used it to collaborate with my friends/colleagues on some personal projects. My only gripe is that formatting the descriptions of cards on the mobile version is different than on the Web version, and any formatting you do on the mobile version will not appear correctly on the Web version. But aside from that, it is a great app.

I enjoy using this program. I decided to start using it as a notebook for notes. One card per idea. I love the card color options, and the ability to create a notebook cover for each topic. I found a way to make the program management into a note taking management. However, my main concern is when I enter information in the description, even after saving it, it won't always save it in the app. I hope this can be fixed. Now I copy what I type before saving it in case it gets lost.

Def one of my fave apps. To do lsit for myself, and plenty of team org templates to keep my team in check. Couple things to improve. Widgets can be improved greatly, super huge and shows very little. And on desktop is the only way to open planner, inbox, and boards @ same time allowing me to move tasks to my Google calendar. Can't do that on phone or iPad. Automations they work... But it's disorganized, hard to search for certain ones you've made, could be more like zapier if possible.

The fact that not all the power ups are available on mobile just really bugs me. So I'm paying $5 per month for Gold and most of those benefits don't carry over to mobile? Yes I can load Trello in the mobile browser but constantly going back and forth between the mobile browser and the app is a goofy broken experience. For example, repeating car detail does not show up on the mobile app. Also annoying approach to notifications. Notifications should be standard without having to add myself as a member. I use my mobile device more than my computer. What don't companies get about this?

I loved this app. Today the app stopped syncing. I cleared the cache, reinstalled and completely lost access. The login screen would open a web browser, would take at least 2 tries then wouldn't log into the app. No way to get support. It wouldn't even let me ask a question in the community, just looped me through endless logins that always took me back to my home page. I know I'm on the free version but no customer support at all is a terrible business decision.

Trello is the most useful task app I've used - and I've tried hundreds. I use it for the video game I'm developing, and I also keep pet records in it for my cat and horse. Everything is neatly organized and plenty of options come with the free plan, but I'm going to sign up for a paid plan to support the devs. The only issue is that the Description field has a very short character limit, so I have to break my information up into multiple cards. Hard to see everything that way.

The app is solid and a decent experience overall, but if you want full functionality skip this app and log in through your browser. For example, you can't mirror cards in the app (a big part of my workflow), but it's possible in the mobile browser version. If that's not wholly important to you and you're looking for a Trello experience that has accessible buttons, give the app a try.

Update: They fixed it! for my personal use as a to do list, it's great. ... Latest update moved "copy list" to the top of the menu instead of "add card," like it used to be. this is incredibly frustrating and almost a deal breaker. I used to be able to add cards quickly on my phone. Now I have to read through the list and make sure I pick the one in the middle. Slows it down a lot. who is making copies of lists so often it needs to be at the top?

I really like Trello, and the app makes it easy to check on things when I'm not at the PC. There are two things that would make the app much more useful. First would be dual accounts. I have a work and a personal account, looking around online, I'm not alone here. Second would be a widget that would let me view a chosen list. I think it would be fine to open the app when you select or edit a card, but being about to view and scroll through a list would be a huge plus.

I make extensive, almost daily, use of the checklists. Same set every day. I check off the items as I get ready for my work day, then uncheck them to prepare for the next day. If I could give 4.9 stars, I would. But there is this one slight difficulty: it's too easy to fat finger the check boxes and be put into edit mode. It would be helpful to me to have more separation between the check boxes and the text editing area.

Really cool app that helps me actually stay organized with my daily routines. But I wish the widget options were better. I'd love to see my "Daily to do" list with my cards on my screen without clicking anything and be able to move those cards over to my "Done" list. And the "assigned to me" widget doesn't apply because I use this app just for me.

I don't use this app as it was intended, but I LOVE it. I crochet rather intricate patterns, and the list allows me to make a card for each row with a checklist for each of the steps. It's phenomenal! Thank you for making cross-platform as well. I can access all my boards and lists from my phone, tablet, and laptop, and it syncs my progress across all devices. All with just the free version. I can not recommend this app enough. 10 out of 10 for sure!

Best thing is that there is a free version. You can really do anything with this app, as reliable as Google Drive, Evernote ...etc. new features are being all the time. A great array of backgrounds for boards. I use it for my personal organization. Switch easily between mobile device to laptop. Convenient, suggestions, color coding, tags, a search feature, archive feature. Really a good resource

Great app, for the most part. Nice and versatile, plenty of features, free, no ads, nice UI, and a truly amazing set of backgrounds for projects. I only have a few issues: It would be nice to maybe see the names of tags attached to cards, without opening the cards. It'd also be nice if i could see the descriptions and maybe also checklists on cards without opening them. Other than that, however, Trello is an amazing, highly useful app!

App has worked great for me in many ways. But it drives me nuts that the "add list" button is kept at the end of your lists. Some of my boards have 15+ lists in them, so I have to swipe through them all before I can get to the "add" button at the end of the line. Ah!! Please move that to the front of the line or add a "create list" option in the menu so we can add a list quickly and easily. I can't be the only one annoyed by this. This app is so useful but this issue makes me want to uninstall.

Recent update broke image attachments. I keep various data images in cards. I need to be able to see the title of the images on my mobile device. You can no longer do this. The thumbnails are absolutely unhelpful. I used to be able to flip left and right to go to the previous and next image. This also seems to be broken. I have to use my laptop to use Trello now. This app is useless.

Todoist is much better for a task manager. Tried to use it as a CRM, but it doesn't really work well for that either. I canceled and deleted the app, but I am still getting billed for it. It's been 90 days. Trello claimed it would only take 1 billing cycle. It's been 3 billing cycles. So, I've paid for 3 months that I haven't used the app.

Free version; personal use only. I absolutely love it. I am an inveterate list maker, and this works the way I think. I love my Tech Toys, but to-do lists have always been on paper. And that means carrying around a 6×9-ish binder, or starting Yet Another To-do List! But not any more!! ::HappyDance:: \\0// I have a board for each project area: genealogy, digital maintenance, needlework, etc. And each card is a bullet-point on one of a blue million scraps of paper with The List for today.

I'm a "to do list freak" lol and I think I've downloaded most all of them on Google Play. I have to say, Trello is the ONLY one that has worked for me long term! I can color code, put labels on them, change categories easily by dragging and the best part is I can share my lists with anyone! I could go on about this app all day. Seriously.. Download it.

Generally, it's a pretty useful and versatile app. However, the free version is not as good as it used to be since the most useful free "powerups" (i.e. adding custom fields) now require a paid plan of $60/yr. Price may be ok for families/ organizations with multiple users but it's a bit pricey for a single user (using personally not commercially). I'll still use for now until I find a somewhat comparable free/ cheaper replacement.

One MAJOR flaw - when editing a note, the app won't stop you or even notify if you're over characters and when you go to save, it will just act as normal yet you just lost everything you put in there. Worse yet, the confirm and cancel buttons are right next to each other so you can cancel out everything you typed by mistake anyway. Just have it auto save as you're typing and grant an undo button so we can actually keep our notes.

I've been using trello for many years and I've loved it for all of those years. However, for the past few months on the android mobile app I can't get the items in a list to order properly. I noticed it when the controls to move list items changed on an app update. Now when I drag and drop, the item flues up to the top of the list. It makes the app unusable.

I love Trello. To date, I've found nothing better. Why 4 *s then you may ask? Two reasons & requests: 1. Bulk Editing (as in moving) of Cards - sometimes, it would be nice to just move a dozen or so cards from one board to another in one action, instead of 12. 2. Better Windows10 Desktop App - the current Windows app is ok, but the Sync is not instant, you have to 'refresh' by leaving and re-entering the board. And you can't have it automatically open on Startup. Please help on these guys !

Tello is a good tool to use, especially working on differentprojects and working with a team it helps with organization and allow everyone to be on the same page, but unfortunately, I received notice that their database was compromised and shortly after my email was hacked. I hope that since that breach, they have improved the security on thier data base if so, maybe in the future, I would use it again.

It was a good app in the past. I found it naturally, because I like atlassian products. Really useful for home use and small projects. And I used it at work instead of paper stickers, collegues found it useful and the company bought a subscription we still use. However, atlassian decided to make freemium version barely usable. 10 boards, stupid workspaces and limits? even my current lists are split a weird way, and I can move them together, even after deletion of few lists to meet limit...

I am a Trello superfan. It is almost disappointingly simple when you first pick it up. The simplicity ends up being its best quality. It's so nice to have an app that doesn't prescribe any type of special format. I have a list for every day of the week, lists for organizing information and lists for throwing ideas to populate those other lists later. It's like sticky notes on a wall -- it'll always be useful.

I am seconding another users comment re checklists not saving. I had a checklist that I have been using for probably at least a year. when I try to enter a new item, I enter text, press enter or tap the checklist, and the item disappears, similar to when checked items disappear because you have "hide checked items" toggled on. I finally had to delete the checklist and creat a new one. note: I probably had near 100 items in the list, so perhaps I hit a hard limit, only without a warning.

The free feature are good, but why do you request permissions to link the app to gmail/outlook and then request a subscription to actually use the feature. In term of security, thats a failure to me. Let me know before I go on and link the app to other accounts that I need to pay for the feature. Its really anoying.

Prior updates fixed basically all prior issues with the app. Unfortunately, now I am finding it failing to sync my boards—on mobile data or wi-fi, latest version of Android, latest version of Trello app, gen 1 Pixel Fold.

Trello is an outstanding productivity tool that keeps my tasks and projects perfectly organized. The board and card system makes it easy to plan, track progress, and collaborate with others. It’s clean, intuitive, and works smoothly across devices. I love how simple it is to move things around and update details on the go. Whether for personal goals or team projects, it’s reliable and incredibly helpful. 5 stars! ⭐📋

please! add the ability to use card repeaters on mobile devices. why I have to get on my computer to add a simple function that should be able to be done on the mobile app is beyond me. I only use this app on mobile.

has been good to use all these years. but with most recent update. my boards from 2 years + with checklists no longer show the check list item. the main cards shows unchecked but when you go in everything is blank

over the years the app was great, we would use it for grocery lists and other planning tools. Randomly i will get an email telling me to "log in" or all my trello data will be erased because my account has been innactive for over a year (which, how if i used the trello app weekly). to top all this off, today, it forced me to "log in" to the app, i assume after they did an update, and now all my boards and connections for grocery lists etc completely gone........

Can't do task reminder notifications. 🤷🏻‍♂️ I had to bounce over to a different app for my project management because I would create tasks and wouldn't get notified come to find out this app cannot give you real-time notifications even though you go in and set it up and it appears that it would. It's unfortunate because I would be a lifer otherwise.
Trello is simple to setup for any company and also good free features with helpful trello selected power ups, try, give it a time to get to know more about it and experiment it yourself. Thank you!
The only way to turn of haptic feedback is to disable vibrations completely system wide as non of the sliders affect it and theres no settings for it in the app or in the notification page

I've lost all of my boards. I thought it was a glitch so I started new ones then lost those. I'm the admin and only one on the account but it keeps kicking me off and telling me to contact the admin (myself). It would probably be great if it worked properly but I'll never know.

great app overall, but now it doesn't update across devices. I literally have 2 devices open in the same board with different versions of cards and one of them doesn't update even after several minutes. this can ruin workflow
hello! love this app, it helped me stay organized and focus on my goals and day to day task. it also allowed me to track my progress ^-^ It's so fun to see my progress ^-^
Great app! just need notifications for the checklist items! And it needs better public board suggestions , leaderboards, focus timers !
Simple and visual tool for managing tasks with boards and cards. Easy to use, but advanced features need a paid plan.
Trello is easy to use and great for planning tasks. Highly recommended!
I like the Trello app because it helps me keep productive. I can collaborate with my employers or contacts through it, managing different projects easily

Trello really helps me stay organized and manage my tasks easily. The layout is simple and visually clear which kinda mek it aesthetic. I like dragging tasks around to track progress. It’s a very useful app for productivity and planning for me.

"login to contact support" are you serious? logging in is a nightmare. where does the authenticator code go? why can't I scan the passkey with my phone? why can't I call or email anyone? I already reset my password and now I have to use 2FA that was never set up in the first place, and I can't login to contact support. this is some Lumen level nightmare bs.
very nice app 😊 . Best for management of tasks. It's helping me overcome a bit of procrastination and manage things being an adhd person.
My experience with Trello has been very productive. The board and card system makes organizing tasks simple and clear. It helps me manage my work efficiently. Very user-friendly.

this is one of the best designed apps I've ever used. it's super intuitive. and it maintains its state, even when offline, pushing and pulling only what's changed. it's perfectly designed. it's super useful. and it could be useful for anybody without the need. for special training. you can basically just start using it. this isn't just one of the best apps on my phone, in terms of its design, but one of the most useful as well. I should have reviewed this long ago.

Biggy UI and doesn't have auto save. Imagine typing a paragraph in the description and then switching apps to another one for reference or closing it and switching notes thinking it's saved and then realizing it's not because you forgot to press the green checkmark. So stupid. Luckily, I spent most of my time in a web browser. But if I had to rely on the mobile app, I would pull my hair out.

The best app so far!! I would add a detail- for some cards to have an option for recurring action, for example to mark the same day during the entire month. And I would add that finished cards go immediately down, so the TO DO cards stay at the top.

Quick-add keeps appearing, setting is not kept. Could you please keep my setting, not to see the quick-add !? Very annoying to hide again after every update. Reduced to one star as my settings keep being reset with each update. Very annoying.
I love the ui, it reminded me of pinterest but in a to do list boards. I can organise areas of my life & random ideas on it.

The web app is amazing, the mobile app is lacking and missing critical functions. How come on the mobile app I can't seem to copy a checklist easily from another card, for example? Am I the potato or are you guys just trash at UX? These little things are making me want to try a new service and I've used Trello for years now

Trello is amazing for student collaboration. My daughter uses it with her classmates to divide tasks for group presentations. They can see exactly who is working on what, which prevents any confusion. It’s a great tool for building teamwork skills.

It used to be great, but they ruined it! I freelance, run a small business too and Trello was great to help me keep all the information together and easy to find, to schedule everything and be always on top of all my tasks. Now I have the boards all split in a weird way, I can't find anything quickly anymore and I refuse to pay solely to have all of them in one place. Forcing users to pay for something that had been free since forever and actually pretty BASIC is absolutely disgraceful.

This used to be my favourite app. It could be used for anything, from grocery lists to project management. The trick was that it had a simple metaphor - piles of cards - and nothing more. Everyone understood it, I could send an invite to customers and they would start using it without any questions. The UI was so easy, you could manoeuvre it with one hand without looking. That was until Atlassian took over and turned it into a horrible mini-Jira that was too bloated for grocery lists and too generic for project management. If I could, I would pay a lot for a 2015 version of Trello.

Amazingly useful! I've been using it almost daily for the past year, it's how I organise everything now. It would be cool if you could turn on an option to have a tickbox automatically added to every card you make, so that you can tick each task you complete by touching a box on the front of the card. Manually adding a checklist to each card, and then having to open up the card details to tick it off is very time-consuming and frustrating!

Overall, I love Trello, It's wonderful. But a major bug made me not trust it anymore after losing my data twice! I reported the bug, but it wasn't fixed. I always use the app on my phone, Galaxy s22, and work fine until I use it from the computer (Mac book pro), and then bad sync occurs. The card that I opened in order to add info to it, went back and displayed old data from 2 months ago! I lost very important data, again! Very frustrating 😕

I like Trello and I use it to organise much of my work. However, very often the syncing betwee devices does not work properly. I use it on my desktop, phone and tablet. Often when I open cards on my phone or tablet the description, comments and attachments are not visible there while they are on the card. Kind of an essential point of failure.
App is the best organizational tool I've used. I like how easy it is to customize and how you can email it tasks on the fly, which places them in your inbox to be managed as needed.

Trello is an easy-to-use project management tool. Its board-and-card system makes organizing tasks simple, whether for team projects or personal goals. The drag-and-drop design is clean, and you can add labels, due dates, and checklists for extra flexibility without making things complicated.

Have been using this for years. I make notes on writing projects, collect details for travel plans and events, maintain lists on everyday to do or shopping needs, as well as annual activities. I also use it for checklists for long running and reoccurring projects and activities. It's basically my external memory for stuff that would leak out of my head if I didn't write it down somewhere. It keeps me from having to track things in a notebook. Very satisfied. Great functionality.

So, so useful for - everything! Apartment hunting? Need to keep track of things to do or buy? Need a checklist? Or to add photos? And, maybe you want let others add or edit your things? Trello was the solution. I totally love it. A simple but powerful idea - cards that live on and can be moved between, boards (card collections). The App and website are easy to use, intuitive actually. I can't rate it highly enough.

Some of my cards don't appear in the app version but in the desktop version they do. Every time I open the app, new cards disappear and other ones (that wasn't visible before) become visible. It's pretty confusing and at the end, I get myself having to check on the desktop version if the next card is really the next one on my schedule, or if there's another one hidden in the middle. If I'm gonna have to check on my desktop every time, what's the point of the app?

Antiethical company. I have many important annotations and now I can only access them if I pay. Ridiculous this situation!!! It should be necessary to send me a notification by email or not allow to update anymore the lists, it is what a serious company should do.

it's nice app easy to work here .. my only concern is notification.. I don't get all the notifications and thats how I miss some of my important project status info. kindly resolve the notification part rest nice app
Trello is simple yet powerful for task management. Boards and cards make organization very clear. Great for both personal and team use. Boosts productivity effortlessly.
Board hasn't refreshed in weeks. I can't see new cards. I've tried updating and clearing cache and nothing has worked.

What I write disappears!! If I get a call mid note or mid sentence, or I minimize and go to another screen for more data, or even if I'm just writing a long note and scroll back up the page before saving everything I wrote disappears. If I write it, I want it to stay. There's no prompt message to ask if you want to discard or save note, just everything disappears. IT'S VERY FRUSTRATING!!

I have been using Trello now for probably 5+ years, and it's hard for me to imagine getting by without it now. For a long time I tried to find ways to store information so that I could access it across multiple different platforms, and while also having the format to allow me to store lots of different types of information - To Do lists, shopping lists, study plans, work notes, diet tracking, etc. With Trello I can almost always find ways to track things quickly and painlessly.

I love Trello, and use it religiously on my office PC.  I'm nonetheless frustrated that some of the basic features of the web version are not available in the App.  More than anything, I'd like the ability to ZOOM in and out.  Trello is a visualization tool.  Only being able to view two cards at once on my more than capable Note 4 screen is a serious limitation in my mind.  Images are also cropped to fit a certain aspect ratio, further hindering visual practicalitiy.  Five stars when these issues are fixed.

The truth is that it's not as good as the desktop version, making configuration pretty difficult (not that Trello has intuitive editing for the desktop version to begin with unless you use Bulk Actions). Having to mark a task as completed requires you to go into the card and check the checkbox beside the due date. The app works and shows you the due dates well but I don't think power-ups are supported at all! Let's just say that quite a bit of things are missing.

I love Trello app. Really. But I have a suggestion. Maybe you should improve the app UI because it looks too... old (?). Like, having more shapes & themes ?? I don't know how to describe it but I hope you understand.
Trello makes managing tasks and projects easy with boards, lists, and cards. It’s simple to use and helps keep everything organized. Great for personal and team use.

It's really clean, easy to use, practical, and I love using it to plan out my stories.However, when I'm using it on mobile (tablet or phone), there is a very bothersome bug.When you go to write on a card that has a lot of text, and you want to write the information at the beginning/top of the text, the card just automatically glitches and teleports down or even all the way down.Not allowing you to see what you are writing.So you have to go back up to see, but then it teleports you back down.

Zoom out mode is really useful. Almost everything needs to be confirmed, otherwise leaving will not save it, which can be annoying (to be fair some most used stuff remembers the input for a while so you can return and confirm it). Missing a lot of desktop features, mostly add-ons and more permission options. Also the voting add-on could be improved.
4 Stars. • Login is easy. • Layout is similar to Web Ver. • Problem AND Recommendation: Would benefit from a more Mobile Friendly Layout. • Room for Improvement.

Trello is fantastic! So glad my brain now has less to store. Great for projects, to do lists, grocery lists, goals...anything you can think of! The only thing lately is (on my phone) when I'm in a list, then I press the back button, it doesn't reload the board so I can see all my lists, I have to go out of the board and back in again. Otherwise it'd be 5 stars

I love trello web and have been using it for a few years at my workplace now. I decided to switch to trello from keep for my personal lists. This time I'm using the android app. But it seems boards take forever to load. Sometimes trello also claims that there are no boards. Edit: Things have improved. I just had an issue that the app kept sending me to the login page or just crashed when I tried to open. Cleared app storage usage and I'm back in business. No complaints right now so rating 5 now.

Works well, and it's especially useful to be able to synchronize boards so they can be used offline. Have just noticed that the app reports "there are no apps on your device that can open this link" for links in cards even if the link is to a card on the same board that I'm already looking at in the app. Might be a tricky one to fix, but it'd be helpful for it to recognise that it's a trello link and just open the linked card.

Love the app , I use it to organise anything , you must note that you do use markdown ,i found out about it by accident , it makes the cards easier to read and more organized. I have been using this app to write down my plans more consistently than the other planning and organizing apps that I have on my phone. It is easier to get the ideas out of my head into written and organized format with this app. Thank you ,wishing you more success and growth.
trello has organised my work sheet and my documents such a reliable apps
You can upgrade easily to premium from the app. Spent 1 hour trying to figure out how to downgrade it back...of course without any success.

Gosh I can't even login on my mobile app. every time I login using Google, I am again brought to the login screen. I have tried multiple Times and even uninstall and reinstall the app. this is so frustrating.
Each update makes the UI a little harder to use. Move is never what I want when I'm dragging a task. So don't make it get in the way of archiving
There is too much clutter when you open a task. The view from the web browser is much cleaner: just the description plus anything else you've added.
In the widget, please add dates for items dated in the future and also fix it so the sort control (swirl icon) works reliably.
Such a scam, they will auto charge you when you switch the subscription from monthly to annual without noticing anything abt the price or payment term. Recommend not to use this scam tool
Can't login to the app at all. The deep link from the browser just stays on the login screen. Edit: STILL BROKEN after over 18 months now. This is pathetic.
The Android widget is useless. I like Trello but the widget is terrible. Can't customize anything so displayed items aren't relevant. Lots of work needed

Perfect for its purpose Very flexible and mostly intuitive. You can build pretty much any system you wish. There is no right and wrong. I use it with a very specific system for getting clarity in my goals, prioritise them, specify all details as they move through the system and make sure I actually work with the goals I think are the most important ones. The :Focus and action: system. Recommended. If you think Trello can't be very useful for you, look for a good system...

Great app and worked well for a bit. However, sound notifications stopped working leading me to miss most of the prompts from customers. When I engaged support, they kept on asking me to repeat the same basic steps without any success. In the end, they said it was blamed my phone /OS without actually troubleshooting the issue. I'll be looking for a replacement.
Probably the best piece of software that actually solves real world problems.

I like being able to group tasks into multiple lists on the same screen, and the ability to attach images etc is very valuable. However, I wish I could delete individual tasks with a swipe or couple of taps, instead of having to drag all the way to archive. I also don't like having to swipe all the way to the end to create new boards. The ability to use a website to edit tasks on a PC is my favourite feature.

Widget is in desperate need of revamping. Add card widget does not work, or at least not the way how customer would expect...thus badly. I like app interface inside app itself but im not gonna be opening whole app to make quick one-word note everytime. I think for how long i know about this app, it should be, as a product further down the road. (Same goes for website app)
We use this every day, we would definitely suggest downloading this it will improve your time management, organisation and everything over and above giving you more Freedom for life.

Trello is a good app for productivity if not the best compared to similar apps, but: 1. There is no card button like in the PC version. 2. There is no option to choose which board I would like to show on my widget. 3. There is no due date of checklists (many people need it because in many cases, different deadline is the point of making checklists). Thank you if Trello wants to consider my recommendations.

The latest update, spring 2024, has some bugs I need to be addressed. I find it extremely challenging to reorder items in a checklist with the new format. You drag to a location and release but it never goes to that spot, instead it goes to a random spot in your list. Other than that the update is pretty good.

I really like Trello for keeping track of the many aspects of my rather busy life. However there a few improvements I would love to see implemented. I would really like to have cards be repeatable. For example, I have a card for something I want to do each week. I also want to keep track on when it was done. However, if I create multiple cards for the task, I can't keep track of when it was done. If I change the due date to the next occurance, I again can't keep track that it was done, unless I add notes. I'd like to have cards that I could repeat at specific durations (daily, weekly, monthly, etc) that I could check off each time it was completed to track it. Also, I wish the calendar would show all of my boards on one calendar or that I could pick and choose which boards to show on the calendar rather than only one board being visible.

I signed up and made an account online with my computer first, then headed over to log in on my app. I get redirected to a website to log in, but when it tries to take me back to the app, nothing happens. I really wanted to try both sides of this program out as I'm looking for a nice, comprehensive digital bullet journal. If anyone has advice on how to fix this problem it would be appreciated!

Easy to understand, easy to keep lists. Way too many bugs. When creating custom rules, these only seem to be applied 50% of the time. Calendar shows tickets on the wrong days, with no way of correcting it. And much more. Very limited in functionality. Would not be surprised if this app can be oneshotted with ChatGPT or similar, except for the cloud functionality and sync.

Trello is an ideal for freelancers, solopreneurs and small-sized office teams for task management with pre-built templates to set up these boards for quick access. You can assign specific deadlines to individual checklist items, add files, documents or multimedia relevant to the checklist items, and use labels for visual organization too.
Great app for organizing, and managing your to do list. user friendly

I first learned of Trello about 2 and half years ago and have been using it ever since. I use for my weekly/daily task management, and for almost everything else that I don't need a pen and paper for. I love that this is available on all devices and browser based which means I don't have to worry about losing a random post-it note. I just love the functionality and it really works perfectly for me. Only downside for me is that with my antiquated Galaxy S4, the app is no longer supported...but that's okay, I just use the browser version on my phone and then do most of my initial planning with the Desktop app.

The latest update has ruined the ordering of checklists! Previously you could click and hold anywhere on an item to drag and drop , but now it can only be done at the two horizonal bars at the right edge. Which is very limiting with regard to which finger it is naturally to use. In addition, when you first drag and drop the experience is really poor. When dragging past the top or bottom of the visible part of a long list, it jump way to quick/long, and it's hard to drag to the desired position.
Trello is 📝✅! 😄 Super organized, boards on point, and gets the job done! 👍
UX is horrible. I can't tag people while commenting on attachments.
App is not syncing..

Tried to use Trello just to keep track of a personal to-do list. I couldn't edit descriptions and when trying to add an item to the checklist the card would only let me edit the card name. Maybe I could figure out how to use this eventually, but it was frustrating enough that I have no desire to try more.

My wife and I use Trello for lists around the house, rather than sending countless texts back and forth, it's great and we've been using it for nearly 2 years I think. Two glaring features I can't find that seem like such no-brainers I wonder if I have just missed them...alphabetizing a checklist, and searching with in one. (We both have Samsungs, Note 8 & gs9).

Unfortunately my new phone S25 Ulta doesn't like Trello. It keeps removing it from my phone..I've tried all the recommended solutions for installing Trello, but each time I close out of it, it's dropped off the system. very frustrating as I need Trello for work. Fortunately it's still working on my old phone J5Pro phone. My sister is also having problems installing trello on her S24 phone.

Not suited to students' needs. Used to be a good app. But now all Personal Cards are being shifted to a separate category they call Teams. You can't create your own Personal Card! Teams can have only 10 cards unless you're paying for a monthly subscription. In other words, free users can have a maximum of 10 cards. As a student, I can't afford subscribing to a professional plan. Time to shift to Microsoft To-do. Have heard it has improved a lot.
perfect for planning you travels and work

I like Trello. It has a great look and is simple to use. Works across devices too which is great. The reasons for 2 stars are: 1. No option to delete directly. There is an archive option, then maybe a delete. Time waster, not time saver. Please add delete option to cards, boards and lists. 2. Even though I have a due date and reminder set, there is never any pop-up or notification for said reminder. Please fix this issue. Other apps give me reminders, why can't Trello?

Great app. Would give it 5 stars if it would automatically make me a member on cards I create, or at least offer the chance to assign members on initial creation. To add to the problem, the widget only displays cards that you are assigned to so if you create a card from the widget you can't see it listed until you go into the main app, search for the new card by name and assign yourself, and then it finally appears. In general more default settings for new cards would really help.

Awesome how you you're not able to login. They use different password for different points of login for some reason. Don't send an email with recovery instructions if you push the button for recovery instructions. They do, however, have the "safety" of having to wait longer and longer at every login attempt. Oh, and the support button just disappears when you click on it. Just perfect. If you don't want me to use the app, that's fine. Byeee.

The app is helpful and well laid out. Recently, on my phone (Galaxy M31 - Android 12) there is a glitch occurring where adding a new card to a list, makes the previous list "window" reduce in size so I can't see all the cards properly. It's a bit annoying as I use the app to see my to do lists at a glance. I have to leave the board and go back in to refresh. I went looking for a way to report the bug and found the process confusing. They say PLEASE report any bugs but it's certainly not easy!

This is a great app. I have been using it for a month now and it helps me to write my stuff and schedule things.Four things I would like you to add: 1. Changing text colour. 2. Deleting the cards option as for now we can only archive or have to reuse it. 3. Adding photos from our gallery as board background. 4.To add a notificatiom or alarm system to remind us of the job that we assigned

In they mobile version... If you add a card you also have to add a list to it. If you don't have a list then you can't add the card. The only way out is to not add the card and loose all the info you put on the card. Makes the mobile version totally useless. The help section(like how to add a list should have a search. Going back to the main screen, there no way to add the link list so you can add a new card. It looks like this is going to be difficult.... Which isn't why I use an app.

Everything else is fine but the app doesn't seem to have even basic reminders... I want it to remind me everyday to check the grocery list but I only see a due date and a one time reminder tied to it, with no option to make it recurring. Can you guys please tell me after you add this feature? xD I'll use this app after you do!

If you are writer or someone who wants to keep a track of things, Trello is a must. Do you have a list of places that you want to visit, recipes you want to try, Trello will take care of everything. I love using the desktop version than the app. Its beautiful and feels like a personal space as I can customise it as I want. It can be a difficult to understand at first and you may have to give it some time.

I have been a one year user of trello. A bit bothered by the lack of customization but here it came! I was surprised by this you are now allowing us with the freedom as well as being able to use power ups! This discovery really made me happy in my productivityyy!!! I am still trying to learn all of the available feautures of trello, I just love the simplicity of a kanban-based system yet I also love having customizability. Well done, trello team!

I use trello for personal use but I have a complaint, It looks so overwhelming when I open any card and i see all the open options. Trello look can be more minimalistic if in every card all the labels, members, start date and end date in quick actions and that too must be hidden all the time and only visible when in use. So pls do this and I will give it 5 stars...

This is a great organizing tool. There are many tutorials online if you'd like to learn the ins and outs. There are many extra features (connect with Google calendar, Evernote, etc). You have access to several of these of your choice, but have to pay once you are using 3.

Can't give more than two stars because since it was bought by another company the app has gone downhill. Doesn't sync properly all the time, and now for the second time in a month or so the app is down and I can't access my lists. Very frustrating since I've used Trello for years and now have to say goodbye and go to another app.

Hey. You did a great job making Trello. I really like using it in my daily routine. For every project I make a board in Trello. But there is a little detail. I use a vidget to see all of my cards. And I don't like tapping button to reload the list of them. Please make that list on vidget automatically reloading, that will be very useful for me!

Frequent bugs. Drag n drop sorting doesn't stick and it'll move cards back to a different order. Yes I'm online. Sometimes says I'm offline when I'm not. Trouble downloading attachments, or hangs requesting attachments. Yes I'm online. Every other app is working but frequently Trello is not.

Trello is a terrible and garbage platform. It's not good at ANY of the things it claims to be good for. It's clunky and the app version is even worse. I must close and reopen the app numberous times to get cards to download and sync. Sometimes it doesn't even sync until hours later. No one should use Trello for anything. There are better platforms for project management. There are better platforms for keeping lists or tasks. The people who like Trello are insane morons.

Used to be my favorite organization app and preached it to everyone. But the more I tried to customize, the less I liked it. And now that I can't make personal boards anymore, even though I never used Trello for business, I've used up my free boards (which used to be unlimited), I've lost my reason for staying. Hope yall can find better ways of making money next time.

What makes people use pen and paper still? It's reliable and easy. I don't have to log into a sheet of paper and jump through many steps to start writing something. And when I grab my notebook later someone has not changed it. Every extra step you add makes it less likely to be used. Forcing everything to work groups that have a limit. Shady move I have a lot of boards that I can't fit now. Trash software trash price. Guess it's back to spreadsheets for me.

It's pretty good actually but there is no option to delete right away not even boards. You gotta make some clicks to find the option if any. I think that people must be aware if they are deleting something first. Maybe you can put a pop-up requesting for confirmation in deletion or perhaps you can use something similar to a trash bin, and people can go there to find what they unintentionally did. It's really irritating to have cards with no use and send them to archive always.

Best app to deal with things to do, except for one thing: even if I had an Atlassian account, I couldn't log in my Trello account after switching device 'cause my other one is broken. :(. When I tried, it said I didn't even sign up to Trello. I had to remember almost every single board I created. Overall the app works very well.

Great app - but why the hell are some changes not saving??? This has the potential to become a really big deal-breaker for me! If I put important information into a Trello card I want it to be safe and not worry about the information getting lost later that day because Trello didn't save the changes!

Just downloaded the app but won't let me login. Says username doesn't exist. I used the web browser on my phone instead and logged in without problems. Tried resetting the password through the browser and went back to this app but it still says the username doesn't exist. Going to uninstall it and just access it by web browser from now on or find a different software instead. Shortest lived app I've ever downloaded lol.

Trello is my favourite organizational app - and it's free! I use it for work, lists of movies and books, and also planning out my goals. Sync between my laptop and phone is great. It's easy to use, fun to customize, and can be used for so many things!

Trello was my favorite tanban app. Occasionally I might have a team, but like many people, I enjoyed Trello for personal use. No more. Unfortunately, it is no longer advantageous to have a free subscription as they have continuously scaled back the benefits. Moving on. UPDATE: I experimented with Asana, Airtable, Wrike, and Clickup. Asana replaced Trello, offering unlimited boards.

3 stars for effort. The main problem I have is that this app is a lousy project photo manager which is an ever growing problem for EVERYONE as pics get bigger and more abundant. Your images are not easy to scroll through, the rotation is often wrong and it's hard to relate notes and comments to the appropriate images. To add insult to injury it now costs $5 month to add images which are larger than a midrange cell phone would take. (Not a problem if photo management was trello's STRENGTH....)

I LOVE this app. Seriously changed my life. Thank you for adding the lower level plan. I tell everyone I know about Trello. I just have one issue. The card covers aren't syncing. I can see all if I use the webpage option. In android phone just now they were there, I freshed and they all disappeared. In iPad, it is hit or miss. Seems like the device I add it on stays, but otherwise not so I'm rarely using the apps for this reason. Thank you fur listening.

Trello organizes my life! The best app on my phone. Edit: Your recent edits slowly remove markdown options from the desktop version and the most recent version on mobile seems to no longer support markdown in edit mode. After saving, it messed up a quite detailed structured document I wrote over weeks! Please allow for markdown edits again!!

I use it for a webapp project that I develop on my own and it's great. Allows me to improve my workflow and organise thoughts and everything. For my project, I had to configure several boards (for each aspect of the development work). One big improvement could be to add the possibility to create blocks of cards in the lists to regroup and colorize. This way, it could allow to have a clear view of the big picture by one quick look.

It's good to keep organized. However, there are a few glaring issues that just make it more of an inconvenience to use. The first being that the app is a pain to get to update when a change has been made to a list. Whether that is on the website, desktop app, or another phone. It takes forever and multiple refreshes to get it to pick up the update. The second being the widget. It doesnt update until 50 taps of the update button. Uninstalled and reinstalled and same issue, support is no help.

Wonderful app, my only complain is that, the app would seemingly lose connection, while my internet is working just fine, like the other apps are working perfectly, but I keep getting the "it looks like you're offline", but the even bigger bummer, is when the app gets back online and syncs, and it completely erases all the work I did on my desktop, it would just replace it with the old version, it really makes me sad.. I really love this app, but this problem is just :(

before the update it somehow started to get restricted. I updated on Note 8 nothing changed. it doesn't allow anything over 10 characters or less even don't even know the exact amount. this stinks!! WTH??? I have all my lists on Trello and now I can't edit basic texts this doesn't begin to make one ounce of sense. FIX IT PLSS. I can't afford to change apps all over again now. very dissatisfied & disappointed. and also please add a search in card option, and the keywords should highlighted.

I've been using Trello for years and was perfect for my purposes both business and personal. Not much use now I have to pay for more than 10 boards and the cost for mere 'powerups' is not worth it without additional boards. I made the mistake of upgrading to Trello Gold before these changes were made. Money wasted. I'll be looking for another solution. Thanks Trello, but no thanks.

its easy to use. The ability to have multiple users access to the one board makes project management easier too. Occasionally I have trouble with the camera not uploading photos and when I go back to the app I have to take the photo again, I'm not sure if this issue is from the last update or when ColorOS updated.

With the widget working as expected, this is one of the best productivity apps I have. Original review: Generally I like trello, but for some time now most of my cards have been stuck "loading" in the 3x3 widget. I just realized it's because of the label; the only cards that load are ones without a label. Anyway, this is a fairly huge bug, and renders the app next to useless for me.

I use Trello on my desktop, usually, but it's miserable on mobile. It logs me out constantly, and any time I've logged back in, it's been a crapshoot as to if it'll actually do it. Today, I tried to reset my password, and after going through all of it, the browser tab it opened simply would not budge. I try again, same issue. What's the point of an app if it can't handle basic functions without opening your browser? This craters my desire to even try Trello's paid features.

I really want to like this app. I like the PC version, but SO little of the stuff I need as a would-be novel writer is in the Android version, that I can't see the value in taking the time to learn either. One Note, even with all its warts is still a better multiplatform app.

I've been a trello user for around 10 years and find it very intuitive and to organise my ideas, to-dos and shopping list. Since the most recent update however, there's a bug where checklist items won't order correctly. Moving an item in a list generally just sends it to the top which is incredibly annoying. I'm on a Pixel 6a.

Love Trello and use it a lot on my computer, but the widgets don't work for mobile. The select button is greyed out regardless of whether you've entered text, attached a file, or both. Will definitely change to 5 stars once this is fixed, so hoping support can reply when it is so I can test it out, start using it, and change my review.

Need a widget to view my In Progress list, to get a quick view on updates without having to open the app... There could be many useful widgets for Trello, they are so underestimated... The app might not be great for moving or editing (better use the desktop for it), but for quick adding cards and checking updates on the go it could be awesome. Check some agenda widgets for inspiration.

I love the fact that I can pretty much do anything related to productivity and structure it the way I want. It does the job of several seperate apps by itself. Edit: I'm migrating more and more things to Trello every day since I can use it for to-do lists, reminders, simple or even long notes, shopping lists and even project management.

The exact app I was looking for!! This is probably my favorite app of all apps lol. It's so versatile it will work for anything. I'm using it to plan social media posts (drafts, ideas, done), business ideas, to-dos etc. All the features like labels, lists, due dates, sharing with others, printing cards on paper...just love everything!

Amazing. The more you use it, the more familiar, the more you exploit it's fluid operation and power ups, the more it becomes the primary source for all things home, business and travel. Our team love it. There are limitations though and an annoying problem where some cards don't sync, so i uninstall from phone and reinstall app to continue.

I find the Trello app for Android to be an inferior experience compared to using the mobile web version in Chrome. The primary reason is the way links are rendered. I use many url web links in my cards to associate with external information. I always configure the links to display either the "inline" or the "card" view so the nature of the link is clear. This works in both the desktop and mobile web browser versions of Trello. However, the Android app will only display the plain url.

Was great but stopped working. It won't even open on my device. I was starting to rely on it and was going to go for the paid version but I can't risk losing everything. It was the first thing to tempt me away from paper back up as was so easy to use but this loss of all organisation in it is going to take ages to come back from

Vast possibilities but Totally ruined widget because of no control nor any logical pattern to which cards it shows (not closest deadlines, nor top of todo list, no possibility to set that up, why have you even made this widget??) EDIT: found it it's the cards youre assigned to it shows was hard to find that out and it's cumbersome to assign and unassign everytime you mark sth done. Bad job!

I use it in my Tablet and I don't recommend using the app here. I've seen how useful it is when I see my friends working with Trello in their COMPUTER, I wonder why is easier in the computer? In my case, I hate the idea of not having the "DELETE" button in the menu when you click in the settings attached to every card, list or board. Developers and owners of Trello, If you want to make our activites easier and help us with your app, please IMPROVE your app to add a simple button that we all need

Can't change text alignment, can't make cards with no text, no way to turn off the little description and attachment icons at the bottom of a card, can't just make the description visible on cards you have to click on cards to see them, tags don't show whatever words you labelled them with unless you click on things, can't simply delete things they have to be archived, there's apparently settings you can't adjust in the app you have to use the website, generally just half-ased. Don't bother.

Annoying that they have deliberatly broken the webpage on mobiles so you need the app. For that alone they can have one star. Edit: to clarify using chrome mobile webbrowser with a pixel2 XL, I can't reorder cards in a list and I can't move them to different lists on the same board.. whenever I try, all I am allowed to do is go into the detailed view of the card and edit it. Very frustrating, and makes the web version pointless- hence my previous comments

The app is called "Manage Team Projects," inferring, they expect multiple users use the same board and cards. It's incredibly frustrating when much of the card info looks completely different for the half of the "Team" that has iPhone vs the other half that has Android. Stop using local phone text code and make a hard code for every device to follow so it looks the same regardless of who's using it.

No notifications. For anything. Sometimes when I log into Trello on the laptop I get about 12 notifications all at once on my phone, but they are random ones. Everything is turned on, battery optimisation is opted out, but can't make it tell me what's due or done. With that being the whole point, it's rather pointless.

Trello is an amazing product that is super user friendly and effective. What is even better is the fact that its equally mobile and PC friendly and so easy to add updates, images, labels etc. There is however one BIG shortcoming, and in my view the only shortcoming, and that is the fact that tickets do not have a numbering feature. If you use Trello with only a few tickets its easy, but the moment you want to use it for medium-larger boards it gets difficult as you cant refer to a number :-(

I love the desktop and mobile website versions of Trello. I fully expected to love the app as well, but it won't let me log in! I have tried everything I can think of to make sure it is not user error or a glitch of some kind to no avail. I can only conclude the app is not compatible with my phone (Samsung Galaxy Note 3).

Really good app! I use it for my freelance business and it's been super helpful so far (especially with how unorganised I am). There's also a lot of flexibility with just the free version too. My only problem is that I would love to make private cards or lists, but a feature like that doesn't exist and working around it can be a bit annoying. Other than that it's pretty good!

Have been waiting for reminders to be fixed for over 3 years now! Sent email to support regarding reminders not working. In the end, I got the standard "we'll let ya know when that's fixed" response. If you're gonna put a half-way decent app out there, why not go an extra step and make sure it works right! And your video ad says it's perfect for managing your life! Not a very good task management app if you don't get reminders.

Using Trello since 2016 for personal use. It's one of the lightest and fastest app which syncs super fast and works offline perfectly. Simple yet powerful. Can be used for task management, but I feel its above that. It's a tool that can manage your entire life, whether it's personal or work. Free Tier is more than sufficient for most of the users.

Super convenient application which I can use on both my PC and my phone. It's super easy to create lists and cards in categories that I create and it's really easy to manage anything I need. I've used this application for my university work and my personal day to day life. Overall, this app is really useful for keeping yourself organised. Especially when you add checking the app into your daily routine. Would recommend to anyone who either procrastinates or just wants to be super organised.

Out of all of the productivity systems out there this is my go to one. My team all have Gold subscription and its the most featured of the bunch and easiest to use. It makes planning and management a breeze. Try searchin GTD and Trello, link it to Slack for collaboration, add it to gmail and Evernote. With an additional Taskclone subscription you can turn Evernote tasks into cards. Download the app and have a play, I am glad I did. Offline or online,it works a treat.

New Trello features, including Inbox, are useless because they're not available in the mobile app. I add the majority of my new tasks via the mobile app "Add card" widget. The new Trello Inbox is not an available option for where to put a new task using that widget. Also, if I add something to the new Trello Inbox on my laptop, I can't see it at all in the mobile app.

Would be useable if you could rearrange/reorder items within the checklist. You can do this at the level of the work station, board, and card, but you can't within the checklist. I was ver y disappointed by this revelation since I spent a great deal of time inputting things at the higher level. Once I was ready to populate my cards with checklists, I realized this app is completely unusable for me.

Nice i mean what can i say it's great with very good features although the marketing is very confusing around the paid subscription i am a tech nerd and i browsed alot to find what i need and i couldn't i had to pay for each individual plan to figure it out. Now this is not about the app what is about the app tho is that it delays alot it doesn't support alot of the add-ons the only thing that i use it for on my phone is to add stuff on the go and browse through my ideas and tasks/to dos.

Love Trello. It's absolutely great for project management. It doesn't export all info when using "Print to PDF. I love managing my website tasks, and it helps me manage my home inventory. Just wish it could export a fully detailed pdf of the full board, lists, and cards in each list. Honestly I wish it could export a plain text (.txt) file of a full board. That would be very cool. Lowered rating because it still does not do these things.

i got to know this app at work. i now exclusively use it to organise my life. admittedly, it's ridiculously overengineered for that. that said, i dont know anything better and also simpler, so i'm fine with it. but i could easily imagine you releasing a "trello lite" which doesnt have all those automated options or at least more hidden. maybe with a tutorial for newbies and reducing needless options. for kids, old phones etc but still using the same account for easy compatibility.

It's nice that the desktop widget lets me see A board. I just wish that it didn't pick a random one that I cannot change. Let me clarify: it is NOT picking the most recent board like it apparently did last year according to posts I've read, it has simply picked a random board that I've used in the past and it's sticking with that one. The rest of Trello is nice though so three stars. 😅

best because of ease of use. I copied data from a checkbox list on mobile, and I thought I'll just create a card and dump all data into card and organize it when I'm on desktop. I was amazed to get a prompt to either create single card or create separate cards for all the new lines while creating the card. I had to come and give 5 stars right away.

Generally a very helpful app but even though I'm running Android 10 it is not always up to date and I have to manually sync. Yesterday it hadn't synced for 9 hours! Can't find any settings to modify sync or mention of how often it will try to sync or make re-attempts if it can't sync. I have loads of mobile data, wifi always on but often can only use mobile data and I regularly enter poor signal areas. It would be helpful if a sync overdue flag was prominent. I have set offline files.

A different way to track ideas/projects and to do lists. I really wanted to stick to Google notes and I tried Microsoft's to do but Trello is the one for me because of what I'm tracking specifically is much better represented in the layout. Even my daily tasks are nicely organized now and since I can only have 10 boards free, I'm more selective about what projects and tasks I put in. My other apps are messy mountains of notes. Don't change Trello, please.

The ui is pretty but user hostile. When you edit things they give you an x to cancel and a checkmark to save. But if you click out of the field, with no warning they throw your changes away. This is the only user hostile app i use and so i frequently make this mistake and it is so frustrating I'm looking for any option other than to continue using this.

Project management at its best! Trello is simply, intuitive and best of all, it actually works! Projects and boards are simple to create and manage. Plus, Trello has desktop apps that sync with the mobile app for seamless editing. And I'm using the free version. And it's not just for project management. I use it for both my online business management and my personal life as a to-do list and task reminder. Look up a few YouTube videos to get started and check it out for yourself. Thank me later!

If you connect a keyboard to your android device the up and down arrow keys don't move through the text, they just jump out of the text box and select other parts of the app. This seems like a really big oversight for a productivity app. When I'm actually using it to be productive it fails me.

Horrible since the changeover. Power ups don't show up in mobile app, all the stuff we previously used that was free is now being taken away and only usable in premium version which for a family to use is too expensive for each family member to have premium. I have to find another way/app to replace this one because the change over to Atlassian has made the user experience difficult to use and expensive. Plus it's always breaking down now and having issues. Miss the old Trello.

Missing many functions from the web version still, like the colour covers, the "convert to link card" ability, Delete closed board etc. Also cannot seem to allow clicking and redirecting from Link cards. But, still a good app. In smaller screens it's far easier to use this. In bigger screens, I'd recommend just using the web version on your browser of choice - a bit cluttered, but offers the full trello experience. 😂

(APP LITERALLY DOES NOT CONTAIN ADS) I really love Trello and has been using it for months hehe it's really clever and convenient and synchs well on my PC and Phone. Though, it only allows you to create 10 boards on the free version. They'd ask you to pay for the unlimited boards. I really had no choice but to remove a board to just add another ): please take this as a sign to improve the system :)) But then, Thanks for creating this helpful software!! I was aided in time managing bc of this!

I use Trello throughout all categories of my life. It stores my to-do list, home renovation ideas, grocery lists, and creative projects. This app is phenomenal. I love the granular way you can break down tasks into smaller and smaller categories. You can build checklists, attach pictures, make comments on tasks, share tasks with others and allow them editing and commenting, and organize your thoughts/tasks in ways I've never seen another apps do.

It took a good amount of comparison to decide on a project collaboration tool, and it was definitely worth the effort. After trying every major and some lesser-known apps of this genre, Trello came out with top marks for a number of reasons. Multiple checklists per item, the very simple ability to rearrange priorities on lists and checklists, and communication and notification functions all made Trello by far the most useful of the competing apps. Well done, keep it up!

This is the slowest app on my MacBook Pro and Android. It has automation rules which made me a paying customer. If the speed does not increase coming 4-5 months, I might downgrade to a basic free plan as the automation I use could be transferred to another better alternative saving me many hours on loading time. The automation is what keeps this app above 3 stars. It's very far from a 5 though. Refreshing screen is also still required. Improvements to search were launched. Development needed.

My old review was 3 stars and I forgot about it, until a friend asked me about it, considering Trello for his project. In the meantime Trello improved considerably, bug I had is long gone, I am using it for my various projects. I like how I can design the cards: colors, tags, cover picture, moving cards around, add ons, and most recently I added Calendar and I love it! All in free version.

i don't recommend this app at all , always with massage " Something went wrong while attempting to validate your credentials. " always !!! i had hard time to sign up than it worked even with the same info and pass so it's not problem in them , than had hard time to login witg the same massage !!! so i had to delete my account even when i was just starting using the app !!!

Since I discovered this app I've been using nothing else. It's perfect for planning the week, the month and the year, or setting reminders in a year's time. I love the finger sensitive swiping when I drag tasks from one board to another, or get rid of them because they're done. This kinesthetic UI suits me! And as a writer, I get ideas at odd times and odd places. Trello helps me nail them down and find then again. So yeah, what a blessing this app is. Thank you team Trello.

If I could give Trello 500 stars, I would. It is simple, makes sense and SO functional. I have used it for years, but recently I was able to completely automate my entire life, courtesy of this amazing app. It may take a little getting used to, to be able to fully open your mind to its possibilities, but once you do, the sky is the limit. I cannot recommend this app enough. The end.

It's great to organise basic projects with a team. The app functionality decently mimics the desktop/web version. Some clunky and limited UI/UX design (platform overall), but not so bad. NEEDS DARK THEME THO! Besides that, a fairly massive downer is that the notifications suuuuck. Should give more info in the shade and have an option for popups. Option to add to cards, instead of just creating new, from Android share shade would also be killer. A few base improvements and I'd probably pay for it.

I LOVE TRELLO.... Until.... The Android App takes a big old dump and won't let me log in- at all. I even disabled my 2FA trying to improve ability to log in. Android app keeps launching a web page to log in. I do that and it returns me to the app to log in, which takes me back to the web page to login. Desktop Web page works just fine- now without 2FA. What's up TRELLO? UPDATE: Reporting issues on the Internet is like navigating 9 levels of hell. Make stuff EASY for your loyal PAYING CUSTOMERS

Amazing! This app what exactly what I was looking for and at the same time I had no idea how much I needed it in my life! I wanted to create a progression chart for our customers as they moved through our service experience and I imagined a whiteboard with columns and post it notes that could be moved from one to the next as they were booked in, then serviced, then given a follow up call etc. But I had no idea this app existed. When I found it 😍

I really like the organization of Trello, I use it to storyboard and outline. But when I use the app I always end up retyping since the card you are working on is erased if you don't save before switching apps or going to the home screen. As I multitask, its a hassle to rewrite the same card 2-3 times when I forget to save. I hope this is fixed, as everything else so far is fluid and good.

I'm using Trello since 6 year free version, it's great tool, I recently switched to MeisterTask and revert back to Trello, though MeisterTask has some great features like time tracking, custom fields, more colorful list which give a modern look. But Trello is top of that.... Don't decide on negative ratings. Give a try and it's really pay off!

I manage my own life with Trello just like all projects at work and I can't live without it. The new zoom out button on android is exactly what I needed, because I have a lot of lists and swiping to get to the ones furtjer to the right is pain. However, swiping in zoomed out mode is almost just as slow as when in zoomed in mode, so it is not as beneficial as it could be. Please make it more fluent.

It's great for what we need it for. I do wish that members did not have the ability to edit info on the cards and only be able to interact with things such as checklists and moving lists between cards. the tiered system doesn't really make sense if members can do the same things as admin.

I have been using this app (as well as the desktop versions on browser and app) for quite some time. But recently, the notifications aren't getting updated frequently enough. Sometimes, some things don't even show up on notifications anymore. I'm not the only one facing this problem. I know for fact some others have this complaint too. I've checked everything in the settings. But there is nothing wrong with the settings.

Update 1/19/21: Originally gave 3 stars in 2018. But, now 1 star after most recent software update. Font sizes are all over the place and app has become unusable. Will be deleting and using something else. ___ Has some very good features and functions but is not yet a replacement for Google Keep because of the limits to data per card. There's a limit on the amount of text that can included in the description and a limit to the number of checklist items that can be included on a single card.
